Params: {3: 'Nikola wakes with an unsettling premonition that lingers all morning.'}
{3: '【kuroname】 is already aboard the transport returning from conservation area 368, the mission report sent to Nikola\'s terminal.'}
{2: 'Nikola', 3: 'In other words, they purposefully send Kolm Lebrant onto Babylonia.', 4: '1'}
{3: 'Driven by the needle-sharp premonition of danger, Nikola quickens his pace, hurrying toward Kolm Lebrant\'s location.'}
{1: '1', 3: 'On his way to Hassen\'s office, Yan Zhen is lost in thought.'}
{3: 'His mind drifts through the ghosts of the Third Dark Room: the hundreds of volunteers, the long hours beside Moineau\'s sterile chamber, a mosaic of faded memories.'}
{3: 'Yet, it always sharpens to a single, searing point: the day Hassen returned to announce the World Government was pulling its funding.'}
{3: 'The numbness, the shock, the pleas, the despair...'}
{3: 'The entire emotional cataclysm has blurred into a dull ache.'}
{1: '1', 3: 'All that remains vivid is Hassen\'s regretful apology and the sight of his retreating back.'}
{3: 'Hassen was a messenger, truly. First, he delivered hope; then, he delivered despair.'}
{3: 'Years later, Yan Zhen found his way to Babylonia. His expertise earned him a place within the very machine that had abandoned him.'}
{3: 'It even placed him across the table from Hassen professionally, but not once did the man mention the Third Dark Room.'}
{3: 'The past recedes as the present reasserts itself: Hassen\'s office door now stands before him.'}
{3: 'It slides open with a soft hiss. Without touching the controls, Yan Zhen guides his wheelchair forward, the machine gliding silently on the path of his thought.'}
{3: 'Just as he did forty years ago, Hassen calls him "Doctor." The title is a fossil, a relic from a world that no longer exists.'}
{3: 'The nearby coffee machine sputters to life. Yan Zhen accepts a cup and takes a slow, deliberate sip.'}
{2: 'Yan Zhen', 3: 'Hmm... The same beans as before?', 4: '2'}
{2: 'Yan Zhen', 3: 'Though the acidity is much sharper now.', 4: '2'}
{2: 'Hassen', 3: 'These are not the best of times.', 4: '1'}
{2: 'Yan Zhen', 3: 'For certain people in Babylonia, I imagine life continues unchanged.', 4: '2'}
{2: 'Yan Zhen', 3: 'I\'ve done a great deal of thinking over the years... What happened then cannot be laid solely at Trillard\'s feet, or yours.', 4: '2'}
{2: 'Yan Zhen', 3: 'The Parliament is a tapestry of conflicting interests. Progress was always an uphill battle.', 4: '2'}
{2: 'Yan Zhen', 3: 'Kurono, Earth loyalists like you and Nikola, the voyagers, the emerging space generation, those upstarts from F.O.S., not to mention the shortsighted opportunists...', 4: '2'}
{2: 'Yan Zhen', 3: 'I imagine your position was not an easy one, either...', 4: '2'}
{2: 'Hassen', 3: 'We always find a way through hardship.', 4: '1'}
{2: 'Yan Zhen', 3: 'Hah, ever the optimist, aren\'t you?', 4: '2'}
{3: 'Hassen opens his mouth to speak, only to be cut short.'}
{3: 'Yan Zhen\'s gaze looks straight through Hassen, fixed on a distant, invisible point.'}
{3: 'His voice is a blend of weary acceptance and the faint, sharp edge of an old resentment.'}
{1: '1', 2: 'Yan Zhen', 3: 'Why did it have to be you who came to the Third Dark Room to tell me the project was canceled?', 4: '1'}
{2: 'Yan Zhen', 3: 'And yet... you were the one who first offered me that hope...', 4: '1'}
{2: 'Yan Zhen', 3: '...Hassen, is this broken world the future you envisioned when you abandoned the one I was fighting for?', 4: '1'}
{1: '1', 3: 'Yan Zhen produces a standard-issue handgun from his coat. He raises the dark barrel toward Hassen, his own eyes just as hollow.'}
{2: 'Hassen', 3: 'Are you sure this is the path you want to take?', 4: '2'}
{2: 'Yan Zhen', 3: 'Back then... was Project Connection\'s emblem a tree?', 4: '1'}
{3: 'A bitter smile touches Yan Zhen\'s lips.'}
{2: 'Yan Zhen', 3: 'You will become the sinner—and I, the jester who cheers you on.', 4: '1'}
{2: 'Yan Zhen', 3: 'You once said anyone could become the President of the World Government. Then we shall see if that\'s true.', 4: '1'}
{3: 'A sound comes from outside the door, but it no longer matters.'}
{3: 'The windmill of fate has begun its turn.'}
{1: '1', 2: 'Moineau', 3: 'Does it really have to come to this...', 4: '1'}
{3: 'Amid the swirling shards in the sky, a thousand roses shimmered in the light.'}
{1: '1', 2: 'Teddy', 3: 'I\'ve been thinking for a long time... Why do you have to erase my memories? Why go to such extremes to play these games...?', 4: '1'}
{2: 'Teddy', 3: 'Moineau. What are you... what are you people really plotting against Babylonia?', 4: '1'}
{1: '1', 2: 'Nikola', 3: '...What do you mean Kolm Lebrant is dead?', 4: '1'}
{2: 'Nikola', 3: 'How many times did I tell you to keep an eye on him?!', 4: '1'}
{2: 'Wheel of Fortune', 3: 'Oh, it\'s Babylonia. A wonderful place that is.'}
{3: 'In the summer of 2155, Hassen and Yan Zhen shook hands beneath the emblem of the World Government.'}
{3: 'They were idealists then, proclaiming that they were to build a world for all.'}
{3: 'Now, years later, a bullet fires from the gun in Yan Zhen\'s right hand.'}
{3: 'It cuts through the night breeze over Kowloong Central University\'s running track,', 17: '1', 22: '1'}
{3: 'pierces the memory of his wedding with Claire,', 17: '1', 22: '1'}
{3: 'His puppets\' assassination attempt failed. They\'ve tracked him down here. The direct attempt on Hassen\'s life is also ruined...'}
{1: '1', 2: 'Yan Zhen', 3: 'It\'s time for that hypocritical banner in the sky to fall. Forgive me for this... but I regret nothing.', 4: '1'}
{3: 'He turns the gun on himself and pulls the trigger again, the bullet tearing through his own heart.'}
{3: 'A sound comes from outside ■■, but it no longer matters.'}
{3: 'The windmill of fate has begun its turn.'}
